body.title: List primitive — formalize append+cursor+index+observer over existing graph
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Many Igor subsystems are shaped like lists but each keeps ad-hoc head/tail bookkeeping: ticket queue, cc_inbox, channel, consults log, session key_changes, memory version chains. Formalize a List primitive over the existing graph (node+edge storage) so these all use one shape. Akien noted versioned memories are starting to show up conceptually in many places — formalizing lists (and by extension queues, rings, priority queues) gives all of them one reusable primitive.

## Design captured from session 2026-04-21a (Akien + CC, 2026-04-23)

**Storage:** always append to the end. FIFO vs FILO is a cursor/read-strategy concern, not a storage concern.

**Read strategies (cursor-as-policy):**
- FIFO: cursor reads head forward
- FILO/LIFO: cursor reads head backward (or tail forward)
- Priority queue: secondary index (heap or sorted tree) maintained incrementally on insert/update/delete; reads by max-by-key
- Ring/window ('last N', 'last 30 days'): cursor rule ('below sequence X = invisible'), not physical truncation. Same mechanism handles TTL.

**Secondary indexes:** auto-maintained via change events on the list (append/update/tombstone). Second tree triggered every time an element is added, changed, or removed.

**Observer bus:** the same change event that drives index updates also feeds subscribers. One bus — anybody can subscribe to individual or grouped activities.

**Additions from CC design review:**
1. Deletion = tombstone in append-only storage; indexes skip tombstoned; compaction later if ever.
2. Writes go through one append-point so observers see a consistent sequence. Index updates and subscriber delivery fire after the append commits.
3. Subscription durability tier: best-effort (transient notifications) vs at-least-once (durable subscriber cursor, survives restart). Declared at subscribe-time.
4. Ordering key is explicit at list-creation: insertion-order (append wall-clock) is default; event-time ordering available for multi-source lists with out-of-order arrival.
5. Reader cursors are durable state separate from the list — a subscriber's position survives restart, stored alongside the list not in the subscriber.
6. Branching and multi-parent/child topology already supported by the underlying graph — no new work needed for that.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery in sprint. Likely new `wild_igor/igor/memory/list_primitive.py` (or similar), plus migration of each ad-hoc list-shaped thing (ticket queue, cc_inbox, channel, consults log, session key_changes, memory version chains) to use it.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first, no-new-memory-schemas (reuse existing graph node+edge — do not add new tables).

**Scope boundary:** IN — List primitive (append/cursor/index/observer/tombstone/ring/durable-subscriber-cursor), migration of at least 2 ad-hoc list-shaped subsystems to validate the primitive. OUT — rewriting underlying graph storage, changing memory_palace schema, full migration of all ad-hoc lists (that's N follow-up tickets per-subsystem).

**Test plan:** unit tests on primitive: append / pop-head / pop-tail / peek / subscribe / durable-cursor / ring-window / tombstone / priority-index / out-of-order-event-ordering. Integration: migrate consults log (simplest target) and verify semantic equivalence before/after.

**Sequence:** after current batch. Maybe also after T-post-inventory — either order works but both are sequencing concerns, not technical gates.
